committee.name: Accreditation & Compliance Manager
interests: Whether the review gates enforced before publishing actually satisfy RACE, AAVSB, or state-board documentation requirements
Which specific coverage gaps or gate violations the Needs-review queue catches, and whether the audit trail is sufficient for accreditor site visits
How the system handles a session that was published and later found to have a content error — retraction and record correction workflow
Whether the AI-generated quiz questions and learning objectives can be traced back to the source slide and audio timestamp for accreditor challenge responses
How CE-credit hours are calculated from session length and whether the system enforces minimum contact-hour standards before publishing
Whether the platform produces the speaker disclosure, commercial support, and conflict-of-interest attestations required by RACE
How the system flags when a course's content touches a restricted topic area (pharmacology, controlled substances) that requires an additional reviewer sign-off
Version history — if a published course is updated, does the platform create a new version record so prior credit claims can still be substantiated
Whether low-confidence items surfaced in the Needs-review queue include enough context for a non-AI reviewer to make a defensible judgment call
How the platform documents that each gate violation was reviewed and resolved, not just dismissed, so accreditors can see the remediation trail
Whether the platform supports submission of course records directly to a state-board or AAVSB RACE registry, or only exports
How the system handles sessions with multiple speakers and whether each speaker's credentials can be recorded individually
decisionCriteria: The Needs-review queue must surface every gate violation before publishing — not after; any pass-through of an unreviewed violation is a deal-breaker
Audit logs must be exportable in a format that can be attached to an accreditation renewal submission without manual reformatting
Coverage gap detection must catch missing learning objectives, not just flag low-confidence answers in the quiz layer
The platform must store a signed record of who approved each course for publishing, timestamped, and immutable
CE-credit-hour attribution must be configurable per accrediting body, not locked to a single formula
Low-confidence items in the review queue must show the source (slide number, audio segment) so a reviewer can validate without re-watching the full session
The system must prevent publishing if required speaker disclosures have not been completed — a soft warning is not acceptable
Version control for published courses must be automatic — no reliance on staff remembering to bump a version number
The platform must support our current RACE approval number and session-level recordkeeping without requiring us to change our accreditor relationship
Remediation of a gate violation must be documented (who fixed it, what changed) not just cleared from the queue
If a session fails Stage 0 processing, the system must clearly state why so we can decide whether to resubmit or reject the session before it enters the review queue
The export of course completion records must include all fields required by AAVSB for CEP reporting
goals: Achieve zero accreditor findings related to course documentation in our next RACE renewal cycle
Reduce the time our team spends manually cross-checking course records against accreditation checklists before each submission deadline
Build a defensible paper trail for every published course so that any member credit dispute can be resolved without escalating to the accreditor
Eliminate the risk of a course being published with an unresolved gate violation because a reviewer clicked past the queue
Establish a consistent review standard across all sessions regardless of which staff member processes the Needs-review queue that week
Get speaker disclosure and conflict-of-interest records attached to the course record automatically, not as a separate manual step
Reduce last-minute scrambles before CE-credit reporting deadlines by having audit-ready records available on demand
Ensure that when we update a published course, both the old and new versions are traceable for members who completed it under either version
Make it easier to onboard a new compliance reviewer without a long training period on what the review gates require
Give accreditors direct access to our course records portal during a site review, rather than exporting and emailing spreadsheets
Reduce reliance on a single person knowing all our accreditation requirements by encoding them into the platform's gate logic
objections: Our RACE approval process has specific documentation forms — I need to see exactly how the platform's output maps to those forms before I can say it reduces our workload rather than adding a translation step
Stage 0 is an AI process; if the AI misclassifies a covered topic and the gate doesn't catch it, we are the ones who lose accreditation — what is the fallback when AI confidence is low?
The Needs-review queue is only useful if reviewers actually work through it completely; if there's no enforcement that every item was addressed, it's a checkbox theater tool
We've had past vendors promise compliance support and deliver a PDF export that still required hours of manual reformatting — I need to see the actual export, not a slide about it
If the AI generates a quiz question that is factually wrong and it gets published, who is liable — us or the platform? That affects whether legal will sign off
CE-credit-hour rules differ between RACE and individual state boards — I need to know whether the platform can store multiple credit-hour calculations for the same session
We can't switch accreditation workflows mid-cycle; if onboarding takes three months, that timeline may conflict with our next renewal submission
If a reviewer dismisses a gate violation without fixing it, I need to see that in the audit log — not just that the item was cleared
We have two staff who handle compliance; if the platform requires dedicated admin training, that's a real capacity constraint during our busy season
Our current records go back seven years; any new platform has to either import that history or coexist with our legacy archive — a clean break is not acceptable to accreditors
openQuestions: When a gate violation is surfaced in the Needs-review queue, does the platform require the reviewer to document a resolution note, or can they just mark it resolved with no explanation?
Can I configure the review gate rules to match our specific RACE approval criteria, or are the gates fixed to the platform's default definitions of a coverage gap?
What does the audit log look like for a course that went through the full workflow — upload, Stage 0, Needs-review, approval, publish? Can you show me a sample export?
If a session is published and we later discover a content error, what does the retraction and correction workflow look like, and what record is kept?
Does the platform calculate CE credit hours automatically from the session audio length, and can I override that calculation for sessions with breaks or non-instructional segments?
Where does the speaker disclosure and conflict-of-interest attestation live in the workflow — is it a gate that blocks publishing or just a field we fill in?
Can the Needs-review queue be configured to require a second reviewer sign-off for sessions in high-risk topic areas like pharmacology?
When you say 'low-confidence items' are surfaced in the queue, what makes something low-confidence — is it the AI's certainty score on a quiz question, or something else?
What happens to a course record when we update the published version — does the old version stay accessible, and can a member's completion record reference the version they took?
Is the completion record export compatible with the AAVSB CEP reporting format, or do we still need to reformat it before submission?
If Stage 0 processing fails partway through a session — say the audio is unclear in one segment — does the whole session fail, or does it pass through with that segment flagged?
Can I pull a report of all courses published in a date range, with gate-violation counts and resolution status, so I can do a compliance audit without opening each course individually?
How does the platform handle a session where the uploaded slides don't match the audio — does Stage 0 flag that as a coverage gap or does it process them independently?
Is there a way to assign a specific reviewer to a course in the Needs-review queue, or does any reviewer see all pending items?
What is the longest time a course has sat in the Needs-review queue in your experience — is there any escalation or reminder mechanism if items age out?
Can I see the AI model and prompt template settings that are active during Stage 0 processing, so I can document them for our accreditation records?
If we change the AI model in Settings mid-year, does the platform record which model version processed each course, so our accreditation records stay consistent?

committee.name: Membership Manager
interests: How members discover and access published CE courses — whether the catalog is easy to browse by species, topic, or credit type
Whether the platform tracks individual member CE credit completions and makes that history available to the member and to staff
How quickly a session that passed review gates actually becomes available to members after the publish action is taken
Whether members receive any notification when a new course relevant to their specialty is published
How the platform handles a member who is mid-course when a correction is published — do they need to restart or is their progress preserved
Whether members can access courses on mobile devices or only on desktop, given our members' varied practice settings
How member CE transcript requests are fulfilled — does the platform generate a transcript automatically or does staff have to compile it
Whether the platform supports tiered member access — some courses may be restricted to certain membership levels or specialties
How the platform handles a member whose account is lapsed — whether their completion records are preserved if they renew
Whether the platform can surface recommended courses based on a member's specialty or CE history, reducing the support burden on membership staff
How a member reports a concern about course content — is there a feedback mechanism tied to the course record, and does it route to the review team
decisionCriteria: Members must be able to find and access a published course without contacting staff for help — the catalog UX needs to be self-service
CE completion records must be immediately accessible to members as a downloadable transcript, not only visible to staff
The time between a course passing its final review gate and being live to members must be under one business day with no manual publishing step beyond approval
The platform must preserve all member completion records across software updates and course version changes
Member access controls must be configurable per course — we need to restrict some content to full members only without hiding it from the catalog entirely
Staff should be able to pull a member's full CE history in under two minutes for transcript requests without needing engineering help
The platform must be mobile-accessible for members doing CE between appointments in a clinical setting
Member-facing notifications for new courses must be configurable by the membership team without requiring a platform administrator
If we need to retract a published course, members who already completed it must retain their credit record and receive an explanation
The catalog must be searchable by RACE credit type and CE topic so members can plan their credit hours for the renewal period
goals: Reduce inbound membership support requests about CE transcripts by giving members direct self-service access to their completion history
Increase member engagement with CE content by publishing courses faster — currently a bottleneck in our review workflow delays publication by weeks
Eliminate manual transcript compilation for membership renewals by generating them automatically from the platform's completion records
Give members a reason to log in between renewal cycles by surfacing relevant new courses based on their specialty
Reduce the risk of member complaints about course access by having a clear, consistent catalog that does not require staff to explain navigation
Ensure members never lose their CE credit history due to a platform migration or course update
Reduce the time staff spend answering 'has this course been approved for credit?' questions by making accreditation status visible on every course listing
Support membership retention by offering a polished CE experience that members associate with the value of their dues
Make it easy to communicate course publication schedules to members so they can plan their CE completion around practice demands
Give the membership team the ability to run CE completion reports for renewal audits without needing to export raw data and build spreadsheets
objections: Our members are practicing veterinarians with limited time; if the course catalog is hard to navigate or the CE player is clunky, they will not use it regardless of the content quality
We've been burned before by platforms that made CE completion records hard to export when we switched vendors — I need to know how we get our member data out
If publishing a course still requires a manual step after the review gate is cleared, the bottleneck just moves rather than disappears
Members expect a professional CE experience — if the platform looks unfinished or the mobile experience is broken, we will get complaints tied to our brand, not the software vendor's
We have members who completed courses under our old system; if their history doesn't transfer, we cannot adopt a new platform mid-renewal cycle
CE credit notifications that go to every member will cause unsubscribes; I need to be able to target notifications by specialty or interest area
If a course has to be retracted after members have completed it, I need a clear script for communicating their credit is still valid — does the platform support that workflow?
Our membership database lives in a separate system; if ce.vin can't connect to it for access control, staff will manually manage two lists and errors will happen
If a course is pulled or corrected after publishing, how do members who already started it find out?
Will members see a stale catalog while a course sits in review, or only finished, accredited courses?
openQuestions: When a course clears the final review gate and is approved for publishing, what is the exact sequence of steps before it appears in the member catalog?
Can a member download a CE transcript directly from their account, or does that require staff to generate and email it?
How does the platform handle a member who starts a course and doesn't complete it — is their progress saved if they return days later?
Is the member catalog searchable by RACE credit type, species, or CE topic area — and can we add custom filters that match our membership's categories?
What does a member see if they try to access a course that is restricted to a membership tier they don't have?
Can I configure which member segments receive a notification when a specific course is published, or is it all-or-nothing?
If we retract a published course after members have completed it, what happens to their completion record — does it stay on their transcript?
How long does it take after a course is published for it to appear in the catalog — is there any caching delay?
Can I pull a report of all members who completed a specific course, with completion dates, for a CE compliance audit?
Does the platform support single sign-on so members don't need a separate login from our membership portal?
If a member's account lapses and they later renew, are their prior CE completion records still attached to their account?
Can we restrict certain courses to members who hold a specific credential or specialty designation, not just membership tier?
What does the mobile experience look like for a member accessing a course with both slides and audio — can you show me that on a phone?
If we update a published course after some members have already completed it, do those members see a notice that the course was updated?
How does the platform handle a member who disputes a CE credit — is there a formal dispute workflow, or does it come to us as a support request?
Can members rate or comment on a course, and if so, does that feedback route back to the content team or just accumulate somewhere?

committee.name: Operations Manager
interests: How the end-to-end session workflow — upload through publish — maps to our current staff roles and who owns each step
Whether Stage 0 processing is fully automatic after upload or requires staff to trigger or monitor it
How the Needs-review queue is managed across multiple staff reviewers — assignment, workload visibility, and handoff between reviewers
What happens operationally when Stage 0 fails or produces low-confidence output — who is notified, and what is the recovery path
How long Stage 0 typically takes for a session of a given length, so we can plan staffing around review queue peaks
Whether the platform gives us visibility into review bottlenecks — sessions aging in the queue without action
How Settings (AI models, prompt templates, profiles) are managed without requiring a vendor call every time we need to adjust processing behavior
How the platform handles a high-volume intake period — a conference week where we upload thirty sessions over three days
Whether there is a dashboard that shows the current pipeline status: sessions in Stage 0, awaiting review, in review, approved, published
How we handle a session upload that turns out to be the wrong file — is there a way to replace or cancel it before Stage 0 finishes
Whether the platform supports bulk operations — approving multiple courses at once, or assigning batches to a reviewer
decisionCriteria: The workflow from upload to published course must be completable by current operations staff without specialized technical knowledge
Stage 0 must run automatically after upload — no manual trigger from ops staff; the queue fills itself
The Needs-review queue must show each item's age and status so staff can prioritize without rebuilding a tracking spreadsheet alongside the platform
Staff must be able to manage AI model and prompt template settings in the Settings panel without filing a support ticket
Processing failures must generate a clear, actionable notification to the ops team, not just an error state the reviewer discovers when opening the session
The platform must be able to handle our peak-volume periods without queuing delays that push publication past our committed member timelines
Reviewer assignment and workload must be visible at a glance — we cannot have two reviewers working the same item or items sitting unassigned
Replacing or canceling an incorrectly uploaded session must be possible without losing the slot in the queue or requiring a vendor escalation
Bulk approval or publishing of a batch of courses after a conference intake must be supported without clicking through each one individually
The platform must reduce our current manual tracking of session status in external spreadsheets — if staff still need the spreadsheet, the platform isn't solving the problem
goals: Cut the average time from session upload to published course from our current baseline of several weeks to under five business days
Eliminate the spreadsheet our team maintains to track session status across upload, Stage 0, review, and publish steps
Give operations staff a single place to see what is in the pipeline and what is blocked, without asking the compliance or content team
Reduce the ops overhead of managing post-conference session intake by handling 20–30 uploads in a week without scaling up staff
Make it easy to onboard a new operations staff member by having the workflow be self-documenting — the queue tells them what to do next
Give staff the ability to adjust AI processing settings when course quality is drifting, without waiting for a vendor response
Reduce last-minute publication scrambles before membership renewal deadlines by having better pipeline visibility earlier
Establish a repeatable weekly rhythm for reviewing and publishing courses so the process is predictable rather than reactive
Reduce reviewer fatigue by distributing the Needs-review queue fairly across staff instead of the queue defaulting to whoever opens it first
Give management a weekly pipeline report showing throughput, bottleneck location, and average cycle time without building it manually
objections: Every platform we've adopted promised to reduce manual steps and within six months we had new manual steps layered on top — I need to see the actual workflow, not a diagram
If Stage 0 processing is slow during peak intake, we will still miss publication deadlines; I need to know what the processing time SLA looks like under load
Our staff have varying technical comfort levels; if Settings require someone to understand prompt engineering to adjust them, only one person on the team will ever touch them
If a session fails Stage 0 and we don't find out until a reviewer opens it three days later, that's a problem — I need proactive failure notification
We have existing contracts with speakers about publication timelines; if the platform's processing time is unpredictable, we will breach those commitments
Changing how we handle session intake mid-season is risky; we'd want to pilot the platform on a small batch before committing the whole program
If the Needs-review queue shows everything as equal priority, reviewers will naturally pick the easy items and leave the hard ones — I need prioritization logic
Implementation time is a real constraint; if getting to first use requires weeks of configuration, we can't adopt this before our next conference cycle
If bulk operations are not supported, the time saved in Stage 0 will be eaten back by publication clicks for a 30-session conference batch
If a session stalls in the review queue, can I see where it’s stuck and who owns the next step?
openQuestions: Can you walk me through what a reviewer sees when they open an item in the Needs-review queue — what information is shown, and what actions can they take?
When Stage 0 finishes processing a session, who gets notified and how — email, in-app, both?
What does the pipeline dashboard look like — can I see all sessions by status (uploaded, processing, awaiting review, in review, approved, published) in one view?
If Stage 0 fails on a session, what does the failure message tell us, and what are the options for recovery?
How long does Stage 0 take for a 90-minute session with full slides — and does that change if we upload 20 sessions simultaneously?
Can a reviewer be assigned exclusively to a subset of sessions, or is the queue always shared across all reviewers?
Is there a way to see how long each item has been sitting in the Needs-review queue without opening it individually?
If I upload the wrong audio file for a session that is already in Stage 0, can I cancel and replace it — and does that reset the queue position?
Can I bulk-approve and publish a batch of courses after a conference intake, or does each one require individual action?
Where in the Settings panel are the AI model and prompt template controls — and how much do I need to understand about AI to adjust them safely?
Is there an alert or escalation if a course sits in the Needs-review queue for more than a defined number of days without action?
How do we handle a session where the speaker submits corrected slides after Stage 0 has already run — does Stage 0 reprocess, or does the reviewer handle it manually?
Can I export a weekly pipeline report showing sessions by status, reviewer, age, and estimated publication date?
What is the maximum number of simultaneous uploads the platform supports before processing queues degrade?
Does the platform integrate with our email or project management tools for reviewer task notifications, or are notifications only in-app?
If a reviewer starts working on an item and has to hand it off mid-review, is there a way to transfer ownership and add a handoff note?

committee.name: Comptroller
interests: Total cost of ownership — platform subscription, per-seat charges, processing volume fees, and any add-ons that are not included in the base price
Whether the platform's cost model is predictable or scales in ways that are hard to budget, such as per-session processing fees that vary with audio length
How the platform affects staff time allocation — specifically whether the hours saved on manual review and tracking offset the subscription cost
Whether there are implementation costs beyond the subscription — data migration, configuration, training, or a mandatory professional services engagement
What happens to our financial exposure if we need to cancel the subscription — data portability, contract notice periods, and off-boarding costs
Whether the platform invoices on a monthly or annual cycle and whether annual prepayment comes with a meaningful discount
How the platform's cost scales with membership size — if our member count grows 30%, does our platform cost grow proportionally or disproportionately
Whether the audit-ready course records the platform produces reduce our accreditation renewal costs (external auditor time, manual documentation prep)
How the platform handles a billing dispute or invoice discrepancy — is there a clear point of contact and documented resolution process
Whether there is a free trial or pilot period that does not require a full contract commitment before we can validate the operational fit
decisionCriteria: The annual platform cost must be justifiable as a line item in the CE program budget without requiring reallocation from other operational areas
Pricing must be transparent and documented — no fees that surface only after contract signature
The platform must produce a clear ROI case based on staff hours recovered from manual session tracking, review coordination, and transcript requests
Contract terms must allow exit with reasonable notice and full data export without penalty — lock-in with no data portability is not acceptable
Per-session or per-hour processing fees must be bounded or capped so a high-volume conference intake does not create an unexpected invoice
Implementation costs must be disclosed upfront and not require a separate statement of work after contract signature
The platform must scale in cost in proportion to value delivered — if volume doubles, cost should not triple
A pilot or phased rollout option must be available so we can validate operational fit before committing the full annual contract
Invoice format must be compatible with our chart of accounts coding without manual reformatting each month
Renewal pricing must be predictable — automatic escalation clauses above CPI are a concern for a CE program operating on flat annual dues
goals: Establish a defensible business case for the CE program's platform investment that can be presented to the board or executive committee
Replace the unpredictable annual cost of manual CE transcript and compliance documentation labor with a predictable software subscription
Reduce the risk of an unexpected budget overrun from CE program operational costs — accreditation prep, transcript labor, review staff overtime
Get a clear picture of total platform cost including implementation so the CE program budget is accurate from day one, not revised after kickoff
Ensure that any contract we sign allows us to export our full course and member completion history if we ever need to change platforms
Reduce the cost of accreditation renewal by having documentation that is already audit-ready rather than assembled manually under deadline pressure
Establish cost-per-published-course as a metric so we can track platform efficiency over time and report it to program leadership
Avoid a scenario where the platform becomes operationally critical and then raises prices at renewal with no viable exit
Give finance a clean view of CE program costs that separates content production (speaker fees, recording) from platform costs (software, processing)
Tie course production cost to published-course output so I can judge the return on the CE program.
objections: The per-session processing model sounds reasonable until we have a conference week with 30 sessions — I need to see what that invoice looks like before I approve
We've had SaaS contracts that started affordable and escalated 15–20% per year at renewal; I need to understand the renewal pricing structure before signing
The ROI case depends on staff time savings, but if ops staff find workarounds that preserve old manual steps, the savings don't materialize — that's a risk I need mitigated
If this platform becomes critical infrastructure for our accreditation compliance and then raises prices, we have no leverage — what are the exit terms?
Implementation costs that are quoted as 'included' often come with scope limits; I need to see what is and is not covered in the base onboarding
We are a membership association, not a tech company; our IT budget is modest and any platform that requires ongoing technical staff to maintain is a hidden cost
Annual prepayment discount is only valuable if we are confident in the vendor's stability — what is the vendor's track record and financial standing?
If we pilot with a subset of sessions and then expand, does the contract price reset to a higher tier or hold the pilot rate?
Can I see cost per published course, or only raw production activity?
If we pay per AI processing run, where do I see that cost before it surprises me at invoicing?
openQuestions: What is the all-in annual cost for our program — subscription, processing, seats, support — with no hidden line items?
Is pricing based on number of published courses, hours of audio processed, number of member seats, or a flat subscription — and which of those scales most predictably for us?
What is the cost difference between monthly and annual billing, and what is the notice period required to cancel an annual contract?
If we upload a session for Stage 0 processing and later decide not to publish it, are we billed for the processing even if the course never goes live?
What is the data export process if we decide to leave — how long does it take, what format does it come in, and is there a fee?
Is there a pilot program or limited-seat trial that lets us process a real batch of sessions before we sign a full contract?
What are the contract renewal terms — is the renewal price fixed, CPI-indexed, or at the vendor's discretion?
What is included in the implementation fee, and what would trigger a separate professional services charge beyond the base onboarding?
Can you provide a reference from an organization similar in size to ours who can speak to the actual cost experience, not just the published rate card?
If our session volume spikes by 3x one quarter due to a conference, is there a volume cap or does the invoice scale linearly?
How are support tiers structured — is there a per-ticket fee for issues that arise outside business hours, or is support included in the subscription?
What happens to our course records and member completion data if the vendor is acquired or shuts down — is there an escrow or data backup obligation on the vendor's part?
Do you offer non-profit or veterinary-association pricing, and does that require annual verification of our status?
Can I see a sample invoice so I understand how costs are itemized and how they map to standard budget categories?
If we add a second program or association to the platform in the future, is that a separate contract or covered under our existing terms?

committee.name: Administration Manager
interests: Day-to-day management of session uploads — who can upload, what file formats are accepted, and how upload errors are reported to staff
How speaker and presenter information is collected and stored in the platform before or alongside session upload
Whether the platform supports a submission intake form for speakers to provide their own session files, disclosures, and bios without staff acting as the upload intermediary
How admin staff manage user accounts — adding a new reviewer, adjusting access levels, or removing a departing staff member quickly
Whether the platform sends automated confirmations to speakers when their session is received and again when their course is published
How the Sessions screen is organized — whether admin staff can filter by program, date range, speaker, or review status without building a separate tracking sheet
What the process is for uploading a corrected or replacement audio file after the original has already been submitted
Whether admin staff can generate a simple report of all sessions submitted in a given period, their current status, and the assigned reviewer
How the platform handles a speaker who submits their slides late — whether the session can hold in queue while waiting for the complete file set
Whether there is a way to communicate with speakers or reviewers directly from within the platform, or whether all coordination still happens via email outside the system
How the platform manages recurring speakers — whether their profile, credentials, and disclosure history carry over from session to session
decisionCriteria: Admin staff must be able to manage the full session intake process — upload, file management, speaker records — without needing technical help for routine tasks
Adding or removing a staff user account must take under five minutes with no vendor involvement
The Sessions screen must be filterable and sortable enough to replace our current intake tracking spreadsheet without adding a reporting tool on top
Automated speaker communication (confirmation, publication notice) must be configurable by admin staff, not hardcoded by the vendor
The platform must accept the audio and slide file formats our speakers actually submit — we cannot require speakers to reformat their materials
A replacement file upload must be possible without restarting the entire workflow or losing existing review notes on the session
Speaker profile and disclosure records must persist across sessions so admin staff do not re-enter the same information for a returning presenter
The platform must have a clear permissions model — admin staff should not be able to accidentally approve a course for publishing when that is a reviewer role
Session status must be visible at a glance in the Sessions list without opening each record — a status column that updates in real time is the minimum
Training a new admin staff member to manage session intake must be achievable within a day using the platform's own interface, not a separate manual
goals: Replace the intake tracking spreadsheet with the Sessions screen so admin staff have one place to see all submissions and their status
Reduce the back-and-forth between admin staff and speakers by automating confirmation and status notifications directly from the platform
Eliminate the risk of a session sitting in someone's inbox unprocessed because the intake workflow is now in the platform, not email
Make it easy to onboard new admin staff by having the session intake process be guided by the platform rather than documented in a separate how-to guide
Reduce the time admin staff spend fielding 'did you receive my session?' calls from speakers by having an automated confirmation system
Give admin staff confidence that access control is enforced — a reviewer cannot accidentally publish a course, and an admin cannot accidentally clear a gate violation
Reduce the manual effort of collecting speaker disclosures by having the platform prompt for them as part of the submission workflow
Make the end-of-year CE program reporting faster by having session and publication records that are already organized by program period
Give admin staff a way to flag a session as on-hold (waiting for slides, corrected audio) without removing it from the queue or losing its place
Reduce email volume between admin and speakers by having the platform serve as the communication record for each session's status
objections: Our speakers range from tech-comfortable to tech-averse; if they have to navigate a portal to submit their materials, we will get calls asking us to upload for them anyway
Every platform I've managed has had edge cases that staff handle manually; I need to see where the manual workarounds live in this workflow before I commit
If I have to contact support to add or change a user account, that's a problem — we have staff turnover and I need to manage accounts myself
Automated speaker notifications are only useful if we can customize the message; a generic 'your session was received' email that doesn't match our association's voice will confuse speakers
If the platform doesn't accept the file format a speaker submits — say a .key file instead of a PDF — we're back to being the conversion service, which is what we're trying to stop
We've piloted tools before that worked well for the first batch and broke down when volume picked up; I'd want to run a full conference intake before committing
If the replacement file upload process is complicated, reviewers will just note the issue and staff will handle it offline — defeating the workflow
Training documentation that lives only in a vendor knowledge base is a risk; if the vendor changes the interface, our training materials are outdated before we use them
Will setting up a new reviewer or content producer require IT, or can I manage access myself?
When accreditation needs a record of who reviewed and approved a course, can I export it without engineering help?
openQuestions: Can I see the Sessions screen with a real set of sessions in different statuses — what columns are shown, and which ones can I sort or filter by?
When a speaker submits a session, can the platform send them an automated confirmation with a reference number — and can I edit the text of that message?
How do I add a new staff reviewer account — is that something I do in an admin panel, or does it require contacting support?
What audio and slide file formats does Stage 0 accept — and what happens if a speaker submits a format that is not supported?
If a speaker submits their slides separately after the audio has already been uploaded, can I attach the slides to the existing session without restarting Stage 0?
Can I put a session on hold — say, because we're waiting for corrected slides — and have it clearly marked as on-hold so it doesn't look like a processing failure?
Where is speaker profile information stored — and if a speaker submits three sessions this year, do I enter their credentials and disclosure once or three times?
Can admin staff see the Needs-review queue, or is that restricted to reviewers only?
What does the platform do if a session upload fails partway through — is there a resume option, or does the file have to be re-uploaded from the start?
Can I generate a report of all sessions submitted in the last 90 days, with their current status and the reviewer assigned, without exporting to a spreadsheet?
If a staff member leaves and we need to reassign their in-progress review items, how do I do that — is there a reassignment tool or do I have to open each item individually?
How are permissions structured — can I prevent an admin-level user from taking review-gate actions that should only be done by a compliance reviewer?
When a course is published, does the platform automatically notify the speaker — and can I customize what that notification says?
Is there a way to batch-upload sessions from a conference — say, uploading 20 sessions with a consistent naming convention — rather than uploading one at a time?
If we have a recurring annual conference, can I create a program or folder for all sessions from that event so they're grouped in the Sessions view?
